session.tool_result:
1→ 1→# Post CI Failure Summaries as PR Comments 2→ 2→ 3→ 3→## Context 4→ 4→ 5→ 5→CI failure summaries are currently generated by `ci-generate-summaries` and stored only in GitHub Actions job logs. When the TUI opens the check runs screen, it makes 4 serial API calls to find and read those logs. The goal is to: 6→ 6→1. Post the summaries as a human-readable PR comment (visible to devs in the PR conversation) 7→ 7→2. Store the comment ID in the plan header so the TUI can fetch it with a single direct API call 8→ 8→ 9→ 9→## Architecture 10→ 10→ 11→ 11→### Comment format 12→ 12→The PR comment contains a human-readable section AND embedded ERK-CI-SUMMARY markers in a hidden HTML comment. This lets `parse_ci_summaries()` work unchanged on the comment body, while devs see a clean summary. 13→ 13→ 14→ 14→```markdown 15→ 15→<!-- erk:ci-summary --> 16→ 16→## CI Failure Summary 17→ 17→ 18→ 18→**lint** — failure 19→ 19→- Import sorting error in `packages/...` 20→ 20→- Fix: run `make lint --fix` 21→ 21→ 22→ 22→**unit-tests** — failure 23→ 23→- `test_foo` assertion failed: expected 3, got 2 24→ 24→ 25→ 25→<!-- 26→ 26→=== ERK-CI-SUMMARY:lint === 27→ 27→- Import sorting error in `packages/...` 28→ 28→- Fix: run `make lint --fix` 29→ 29→=== /ERK-CI-SUMMARY:lint === 30→ 30→=== ERK-CI-SUMMARY:unit-tests ===
... [174 lines omitted] ...
180→ 180→ - PR gets a comment with human-readable failure summary

1→"""Schema v2 plan header operations for erk-plan issues. 2→ 3→These support the new plan issue structure where: 4→- Issue body contains only compact metadata (for fast querying) 5→- First comment contains the plan content 6→- last_dispatched_run_id is stored in issue body 7→ 8→Optional fields (added over time, backward compatible): 9→- source_repo: For cross-repo plans, the repo where this plan will be 10→ implemented (e.g., "owner/impl-repo"). When set, the plan issue lives in 11→ a different repo (the plans repo) than where code changes will be made. 12→""" 13→ 14→import re 15→from typing import Any 16→ 17→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import ( 18→ create_metadata_block, 19→ create_plan_body_block, 20→ find_metadata_block, 21→ render_metadata_block, 22→ render_plan_body_block, 23→ replace_metadata_block_in_body, 24→) 25→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.schemas import ( 26→ BRANCH_NAME, 27→ CREATED_AT, 28→ CREATED_BY, 29→ CREATED_FROM_SESSION, 30→ CREATED_FROM_WORKFLOW_RUN_URL,

... [224 lines omitted] ...
"""Fetch CI failure summaries for a pull request. # === CI Failure Summarization (Haiku) === (needs.format.result == 'failure' || needs.lint.result == 'failure' || needs.fix-formatting.result == 'failure' || needs.docs-check.result == 'failure' || needs.ty.result == 'failure' || needs.unit-tests.result == 'failure' || needs.integration-tests.result == 'failure' || needs.erk-mcp-tests.result == 'failure') - name: Summarize failures All four code sections with complete line numbers are now available. The CI failure summarization workflow orchestrates the entire process: the GitHub workflow job triggers the `ci-generate-summaries` CLI command, which fetches failing jobs, uses Haiku to summarize them, and outputs them in ERK-CI-SUMMARY markers that are later parsed by the plan data provider to display summaries in the TUI.
session.assistant: I have all the context I need. Now let me implement Phase 2 — adding `get_pr_comment` to all 5 gateway files. Let me read them directly now:

... [5 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Production implementation of GitHub operations. 2→ 3→Error Handling Philosophy: 4→ gh CLI invocations should let exceptions bubble by default. Callers are 5→ responsible for handling failures appropriately. Don't swallow exceptions 6→ and return None/False/default values - this masks real errors and makes 7→ debugging difficult. 8→ 9→ Legitimate "not found" cases (e.g., no PR exists for a branch) are handled 10→ by checking the response data, not by catching exceptions. 11→""" 12→ 13→import json 14→import logging 15→import secrets 16→import string 17→from datetime import UTC, datetime, timedelta 18→from pathlib import Path 19→from typing import Any 20→

1→"""Data types for TUI components.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→from dataclasses import dataclass 6→from datetime import datetime 7→ 8→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import IssueFilterState 9→ 10→ 11→@dataclass(frozen=True) 12→class PlanRowData: 13→ """Row data for displaying a plan in the TUI table. 14→ 15→ Contains pre-formatted display strings and raw data needed for actions. 16→ Immutable to ensure table state consistency. 17→ 18→ **Display vs Raw convention:** Many data points have both a raw field (for 19→ logic/predicates) and a display field (for table rendering). Raw fields are 20→ often nullable (None when absent), while display fields are always strings 21→ (dash "-" or empty when absent). Use raw fields in availability predicates 22→ (``ctx.row.pr_number is not None``), display fields for rendering. 23→ 24→ Attributes: 25→ 26→ plan_id: GitHub issue number (e.g., 123). Never None. 27→ plan_url: Full URL to the GitHub issue. None when unavailable. 28→ full_title: Complete untruncated plan title. Empty string possible. 29→ plan_body: Raw issue body text (markdown). Empty string possible. 30→
... [201 lines omitted] ...
56→ run_conclusion: Run conclusion ("success", "failure", "cancelled"). 168→ warnings: tuple[str, ...] = ()
